note: Deterministic fixtures (fixed seeds per RFC 8032, fixed ts). A second implementation claims conformance by replaying every vector: reconstruct the store, run the GOV-anchors.md s3 verification, match authorized + note substring.

vectors.description: 2-of-3 genesis adoption authorizes
successor adopted under rotated policy (lineage hop)
ancestor mismatch is a fork, not an upgrade
genesis set carrying ancestor is invalid
minted 1-of-1 profile+threshold pair rejected (lineage rule, 3/3 blind gate finding)
under with extra blob is ineligible
one signature and duplicate-actor signatures stay below min_sigs
signature by a key not bound in trust config does not count
same record authorizes once keys are bound
embedded jurisdiction mismatch refused before signature work
adoption outside the settlement closure is ignored
rival authorized successors freeze the chain (no tie-break)
key-state under an unrelated policy is out of scope
unauthorized profile cannot expand key-state scope (P1-R)
unquorumed key-state under governance ignored (Warrant s5.1)
quorum-authorized key-state refuses to the warrant CLI
acknowledged rotation (resolved_key_state) proceeds, no deadlock (Gemini STANDARD-gate P0)
id-unsound record cannot extend the settlement closure (Codex P0, Go parity)

because.text: adoption reachable only via forged bridge

subject.note: adoption reachable only via forged bridge

vectors.description: non-canonical (non-JCS) anchor-set blob is refused even if adopted (Codex P1)
blob with trailing JSON after the object is refused (Codex P0, Go EOF parity)

because.text: adopt a blob with trailing JSON

subject.note: adopt a blob with trailing JSON
